subject: About blinds
body:
This is an email from the lady who is doing the blinds for the apartment that she sent to Susana:


Hi Susanna, 
 
I met with Nadia and remeasured the space.  The 2" Silhouette WILL fit into the soffit (tight but we can do it).  The sizes of the shades have change in length.  The cost of the shades = $7925.00.  This include meas/installation.  PLEASE NOTE:  I would like to get back and remeasure once all of the built ins are installed.  ALSO, the bathroom and door shade need to be remeasured.  If the door is opening into the room, then the shade MUST be installed on the door.  If so, this will bring the size/price of door shade down a bit. 
 
The cost of fabricating and installing the drapery (without including any hardware: rods etc.), and using blackout lining:  would be $1100. In looking over my notes, I found that Greg was unsure at the time as to whether or not a "simple track" would be used or something more "high end", thus the difference in the estimate.  $1100 - is the cost of the fabrication and installation ONLY.  This does not include any hardware. 
 
Please forward this information to you client, Nadia Marcinkova.  Also, let Nadia know that I have ordered several "soft white" color samples which are being sent to Nadia directly.  Nadia provided me with the address. 
 
Thank you, 
 
kate 

Sent via BlackBerry by AT&T
body_html:
This is an email from the lady who is doing the blinds for the apartment that she sent to Susana:

Hi Susanna,

I met with Nadia and remeasured the space.  The 2" Silhouette WILL fit into the soffit (tight but we can do it).  The sizes of the shades have change in length.  The cost of the shades = $7925.00.  This include meas/installation.  PLEASE NOTE:  I would like to get back and remeasure once all of the built ins are installed.  ALSO, the bathroom and door shade need to be remeasured.  If the door is opening into the room, then the shade MUST be installed on the door.  If so, this will bring the size/price of door shade down a bit.

The cost of fabricating and installing the drapery (without including any hardware: rods etc.), and using blackout lining:  would be $1100. In looking over my notes, I found that Greg was unsure at the time as to whether or not a "simple track" would be used or something more "high end", thus the difference in the estimate.  $1100 - is the cost of the fabrication and installation ONLY.  This does not include any hardware.

Please forward this information to you client, Nadia Marcinkova.  Also, let Nadia know that I have ordered several "soft white" color samples which are being sent to Nadia directly.  Nadia provided me with the address.

Thank you,

kate

Sent via BlackBerry by AT&T
